videoTitle: What is DevOps? - In Simple English
videoDescription:
Simple explanation of DevOps without the complications and information overload.  -- what do you think? Please share & comment! 

DevOps is a concept with different interpretations and definitions, but when you get down to it, it's all about developers and operations teams breaking down silos and working together to innovate faster. For many companies, the ability to innovate at a rapid pace — responding to market conditions and customer feedback — is a key factor for success. Adopting the DevOps philosophy requires a new mindset, new tools and new skills.

videoTitle: DevOps Engineering Course for Beginners
videoDescription:
What is DevOps in the context of software engineering? Learn all about DevOps in this comprehensive course for beginners with three technical tutorials. You will learn what DevOps is, continuous integration, continuous deployment strategies, and application performance management. Many DevOps practices are commonly used in programming and web development, it is important to understand key terms and technologies. 

This course often references the MERN (MongoDB, Express JS, React JS, Node JS) technology stack. You will get a series of talks with technology recommendations based on these best practices. There will be several programming examples embedded in these talks. As long as you know the absolute basics of coding and the web, you'll have no problem following along. 

⭐️ Course Notes ⭐️

videoTitle: ChatGPT Tutorial - Use ChatGPT for DevOps tasks to 10x Your Productivity
videoDescription:
ChatGPT Tutorial for DevOps - What is ChatGPT? How to use ChatGPT? My opinion on whether ChatGPT will replace engineers
💛  Follow me on LinkedIn and IG for weekly updates   ►  https://bit.ly/2F3LXYJ
💙  Become a DevOps Engineer - 6-month program       ►  https://bit.ly/43nK7ra
💚  Mini-Bootcamp for IT Beginners                                  ►  https://bit.ly/3BRz6To

#chatgpt   #techworldwithnana 

►  This video is sponsored by Firefly 🙌🏼
►  For more infos: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-Lf0
►  Open source tool AIaC: https://eu1.hubs.ly/H02z-GD0

I'm sure you have all heard of ChatGPT by now. It has become a buzzword within days of its release and professionals in all fields, especially in high skilled areas like lawyers, doctors, engineers are questioning whether such AI can actually replace them and work.

So in this video I want to talk about what ChatGPT is and how it even popped up, talk a bit about the organization behind GPT called "OpenAI", which has already created many other machine learning models besides Chat GPT and also explain technically about all that.
And then we'll dive in and actually put ChatGPT to use for some DevOps related tasks.
I really want to see how it can help in generating configuration code for building DevOps processes or different parts of those processes and how well it knows different DevOps technologies, but not just some shallow examples or boilerplate code that I can get from official documentation, but instead also try more fine-tuning and small optimizations in that configuration code.

We're also going to check out an open source command line tool that is built on top of ChatGPT and was specifically created for engineers to generate infrastructure as code templates and more and finally we'll talk about the impact of ChatGPT, the quality and usefulness of such a tool for engineers and whether it will really replace the engineers and to what extent you should be concerned.

53:53 - Tools built on top of OpenAI's API
55:01 - AIaC demo - CLI tool for DevOps
01:01:00 - My opinion on ChatGPT & whether ChatGPT will replace engineers

videoTitle: The ONLY Roadmap to Get a DevOps Job in 2026 | Zero to Hero Guidance | Senior DevOps Engineer | USA
videoDescription:
This is the exact roadmap that will get you hired in 2026. 🚀

Stop following random roadmaps that leave you more confused than when you started. The DevOps landscape is shifting, and simply knowing a few tools isn't enough anymore. To land a high-paying role today, you need to understand the logic, the architecture, and the "why" behind every deployment.

I’m Avi (Vamsi Avinash Gunji). I’ve spent years navigating the enterprise tech world—from my first role at Clarivate in 2019 to roles at Farmers Insurance, The Home Depot, PwC, and Goldman Sachs, leading up to my current position as a Senior DevOps Engineer. I’ve seen what actually works in production and what’s just "tutorial noise."

In this video, I’m breaking down the definitive path I would follow if I were starting from zero today. We’re debugging your career "Desi style"—from code errors to chai breaks. This is my narrative—unfiltered, evolving, and unfinished.

📍 The Roadmap Breakdown:
In this video, we cover the critical phases to master:
✅ Phase 1: Mastering the Linux terminal, systemctl, and Bash/Python automation.
✅ Phase 1.2: Why Networking (DNS/VPCs) and Storage (NFS) are your best friends.
✅ Phase 2: Cloud architecture (AWS/Azure/GCP) vs. the reality of Private Cloud and On-Prem.
✅ Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code (Terraform & Ansible) to save you from disasters.
✅ Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes (K8s)—The core of modern engineering.
✅ Phase 4.2: Building actual end-to-end CI/CD pipelines with Jenkins and GitHub Actions.
✅ Phase 5: Monitoring, Alerting, and Observability (Prometheus, Grafana, Splunk).

🕒 TIMESTAMPS:

00:00 Intro
00:41 Phase 1: Linux Foundations
01:40 Phase 1.2: Networking and Storage
02:48 Phase 2: The Cloud Ecosystem
04:02 Phase 3: Infrastructure as Code
04:57 Phase 4: Containers & Kubernetes
06:18 Phase 4.2: CI/CD Pipelines
06:47 Phase 5: Monitoring & Alerting
07:28 Conclusion: Senior Tip


🔗 Official Resources & Certifications:
Linux Foundation (LFCS): https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/linux-foundation-certified-sysadmin-lfcs/ 

AWS DevOps Engineer Certification: https://aws.amazon.com/certification/certified-devops-engineer-professional/

Kubernetes (CKA) Certification: https://training.linuxfoundation.org/certification/certified-kubernetes-administrator-cka/

Official Terraform Site: https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform

Official Helm Charts: https://helm.sh/docs/

videoTitle: Cloud Engineer vs DevOps Engineer - Differences and Overlaps of tasks and responsibilities
videoDescription:
Differences and Overlaps of Cloud and DevOps Engineer Roles as well as deep dive of Cloud Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

DevOps engineer and Cloud engineer are 2 popular roles in software engineering. But there is a lot of mix up of these two roles and many people and many companies often use them interchangeably. They mix the tasks and responsibilities of these two very often. For example there are plenty of job descriptions for "DevOps Cloud Engineer" or "Cloud DevOps Engineer", which would naturally make you think that they are the same role, but then why would you have two names for the same role? And that's exactly what I'm going to discuss and clarify in this video.


🔗💡  What is DevOps and DevOps Engineer Tasks and Responsibilities

videoTitle: What is Platform Engineering and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
videoDescription:
Platform Engineering - What it is, how it works and how it fits into DevOps and Cloud world
Also how to approach implementing an IDP - internal developer platform.

🔗💚   Become a DevOps Engineer in 6 months       ►   https://bit.ly/3WKUPGf
🔗💙   Become a Kubernetes Administrator              ►   https://bit.ly/3IMJUWI
🔗🧡   Complete GitLab CI/CD course                        ►   https://bit.ly/42itb4B
🔗 💜  DevSecOps Bootcamp                                       ►   https://bit.ly/3OI6y66

▬▬▬▬▬▬ Thank you Pulumi for sponsoring this video 👏  ▬▬▬▬▬▬
►   Learn more about Pulumi's Internal developer platform-in-a-box: https://pulumip.us/IDP-Platform-Engineering
►   Join PulumiUp - a global Infrastructure as Code conference: https://www.pulumi.com/pulumi-up/

In this video we're going to talk about a relatively new hot topic in DevOps and Cloud space, which is “platform engineering”. There is a lot of discussion going on, where some people are asking whether platform engineering replaces DevOps. Many people say it goes hand in hand and is rather an addition to DevOps, but in reality it's a bit more complicated than that. Platform engineering actually changes a lot of established rules we knew about DevOps, SRE and Cloud engineering, it changes the game and introduces some new rules.

So let's clearly define, what platform engineering exactly is, more interestingly, why was there even a need for this new role and how it evolved and of course how it compares to DevOps and Cloud engineering and does it really replace any of these roles?

17:53 - How it looks in real-world?

24:30 - How to implement an IDP successfully
30:14 - Platform Engineering vs DevOps
34:53 - Both Skills in TWN education
39:33 - Platform vs Cloud Engineer
41:01 - Wrap Up

videoTitle: How AI is Changing DevOps Careers | What You Need to Know
videoDescription:
DevOps Career in 2026: Should You Learn AI or Double Down on DevOps Fundamentals?
► Grab your DevOps Starter Kit: https://bit.ly/4oZ4qGD
► Skills Companies ACTUALLY Want: https://youtu.be/7d3MU2DgUPg

In this video I sat down with Bret Fisher - Docker Captain and DevOps educator who spent 2 years walking around conferences asking engineers one question: Are you actually using AI in DevOps?

The disconnect between what people say online and what's really happening will surprise you.

We break down:
- the AI hype vs reality
- what AI can actually do for DevOps RIGHT NOW (not in 5 or 10 years)
- and the practical first steps you can take without getting overwhelmed by the chaos

Plus: why companies still want Kubernetes experts, not AI prompt engineers.
If you're confused about AI in DevOps, this conversation will clear everything up.

videoTitle: Will AI replace DevOps Engineer? AI tools in DevOps 2024
videoDescription:
AI for DevOps | Will AI replace DevOps engineers | DevOps AI tools
In this DevOps video we will learning How AI and ML can be used in DevOps tasks like CICD, Monitoring, Logging, Deployments and more.
Along with Popular AI tools in devops and companies using AI with Devops.

Connect with me on LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/nasiullha-chaudhari/ 

AI and DevOps are two fascinating fields in the tech world, and they're coming together in exciting ways. Many companies are exploring how AI can help improve their DevOps practices to make things smoother and more efficient.

With AI, we can automate repetitive tasks, optimize resource usage, and make better decisions.

A common question is whether AI will replace DevOps or the engineers working in this area. Well, AI can certainly enhance DevOps, but it won't replace the people. Instead, it will work alongside skilled DevOps engineers to make things even better.

Timestamps
Intro 0"00
Eminem Featuring 0:50
DevOps Tasks that AI can replace 01:20
Popular AI tools in DevOps 03:25
Companies using AI in DevOps 04:40
How Netflix AI with DevOps 04:48
How Google uses AI in Devops 04:58
How LinkedIn uses AI for DevOps 05:16
Problems when using AI for DevOps 05:47
Outro 07:26

The GitLab Developer Survey, available at https://about.gitlab.com/developer-survey/ 

AI Tools for DevOps:
1. Splunk
2. Harness
3. Amazon CodeGuru
4. PagerDuty
5. Dynatrace
6. Datadog
7. AppDynamics
8. Opsani
9. Puppet
10. Chef

These AI-powered tools, combined with popular DevOps platforms, enable organizations to accelerate their software development and delivery processes while ensuring greater reliability, scalability, and performance. The integration of AI in DevOps brings innovative solutions to complex challenges, allowing teams to focus on creating value for their customers.

1. Netflix: Netflix is well-known for its use of AI and machine learning to optimize content delivery, personalization, and infrastructure management in their DevOps workflows.
2. Google: Google has been a pioneer in AI research and has integrated AI tools and technologies into their DevOps processes to improve system performance and operational efficiency.
3. Facebook: Facebook uses AI and machine learning algorithms to optimize infrastructure, manage user data, and streamline their continuous deployment practices in DevOps.
4. Amazon: Amazon's AWS offers various AI-powered DevOps tools and services, such as Amazon CodeGuru, AWS CodePipeline, and AWS CodeDeploy, to help developers automate tasks and improve software delivery.
5. Microsoft: Microsoft uses AI in their DevOps workflows to optimize Azure services, automate infrastructure management, and enhance developer productivity.
6. Spotify: Spotify uses AI-driven analytics and monitoring tools to gain insights into application performance and user behavior, enabling continuous improvements in their DevOps practices.
7. Twitter: Twitter employs AI and machine learning for infrastructure management, incident detection, and performance optimization in their DevOps pipelines.
8. Uber: Uber utilizes AI to manage large-scale infrastructure, improve data analysis
9. Airbnb: Airbnb leverages AI to optimize their continuous deployment and delivery pipelines
10. IBM: IBM has integrated AI technologies into their DevOps tools and solutions, such as IBM Cloud Pak for Applications, to facilitate intelligent automation and faster software delivery.

Adoption of AI in DevOps is not limited to these companies alone. More and more companies are embracing AI in their DevOps processes. By doing so, they can reduce manual work, spot issues faster, and deliver higher-quality software to their customers.

In conclusion, AI and DevOps make a fantastic team. They bring exciting possibilities for the future of software development, making it more efficient and effective. Embracing AI in DevOps can give your company a competitive edge in the tech world. So, let's welcome this exciting partnership!

videoTitle: Introducing AWS DevOps Agent (Preview) | Amazon Web Services
videoDescription:
AWS DevOps Agent (preview) is a frontier agent that resolves and proactively prevents incidents, continuously improving reliability and performance. AWS DevOps Agent investigates incidents and identifies operational improvements as an experienced DevOps engineer would: by learning your resources and their relationships; working with your observability tools, runbooks, code repositories, and CI/CD pipelines; and correlating telemetry, code, and deployment data across all of them to understand the relationships between your application resources. DevOps Agent uses this deep understanding of your operations and workloads to reduce MTTR and drive operational excellence.

Learn more about AWS DevOps Agent: https://go.aws/devops-agent

videoDescription:
ทำไมแต่ละองค์กรถึงให้นิยามความหมายของ DevOps ไม่เหมือนกัน แม้กระทั่ง JD ของอาชีพ DevOps Engineer แต่ละที่ยังแตกต่างกันอย่างสิ้นเชิง

ถ้า DevOps คือแนวคิด ไม่ใช่ตำแหน่งงานแล้ว จะหยิบจับใครมาเป็น DevOps Engineer ก็ได้จริงหรือ? 🤫

ร่วมฟังความคิดเห็นจาก 2 มุม 2 สายอาชีพ 👨🏻‍💼 ตัวแทนทีม Software Developer โดยคุณปุ๋ย สมเกียรติ ปุ๋ยสูงเนิน - Technical Coach ที่สยามชำนาญกิจ  👨🏻‍💼 และตัวแทนทีม Operation หรือ DevOps Engineer, SRE, Platform Engineer โดยคุณเดียร์ จิรายุส นิ่มแสง - ผู้ก่อตั้ง และ CEO ของ Opsta (Thailand)

รายการ Career Lab รายการที่สำรวจและเจาะลึก insight ต่าง ๆ ของอาชีพที่ in-demand ที่สุดในตอนนี้ เราจำนำพา Expert ตัวท้อปขมานั่งเม้าท์ฉ่ำกับประเด็นสุดร้อนแรงแบบไม่มีกั๊ก และไม่เคยพูดถึงที่ไหนมาก่อน !!

✅ กว่า 70% ของ DevOps Engineer ในปัจจุบันไม่ใช่ DevOps ที่แท้จริง?
✅ DevOps (อาจ) ไม่มีอยู่จริง จริง ๆ แล้วคือ Platform Engineering
✅ องค์กรจะปรับตัว Transform ในการทำ DevOps ที่แท้จริงได้อย่างไร?
✅ โลกของ DevOps การทำงานจริง ใครควรรับผิดชอบอะไร ทั้งในมุมของทีม Software Dev และทีม DevOps Engineer, SRE, หรือ Platform Engineer?
✅ Team Lead หรือ Tech Manager ควรจะแก้ปัญหา ความไม่เข้าใจกันระหว่างทีมพัฒนาในองค์กรอย่างไร?

💡 อย่าลืมกด Subscribe และ เปิด 🔔 แจ้งเตือนเพื่อไม่ให้พลาด Contents ดีๆ จาก Skooldio

📍เพื่อช่วยทุกคนพัฒนาทักษะที่กำลังเป็นที่ต้องการสูงสุดในปัจจุบันและมีอนาคตที่ดีขึ้น ที่ Skooldio เราเชื่อในพลังแห่งการเรียนรู้ ครอบคลุมทั้งด้าน AI, Data, Tech, Digital Product และ Business 

และเราเป็นจะผู้นำในการพัฒนา Platform สำหรับการเรียนรู้ที่ผสมผสานการเรียนรู้ทั้ง Online และ Offline ให้คนไทยเก่งขึ้นพร้อมทำงานบนตลาดแรงงานอย่างมีคุณภาพ ✨ เลือกอัปสกิลที่ช่วยให้คุณเก่งขึ้นแบบก้าวกระโดดได้เลยที่นี่ https://to.skooldio.com/LQ57gVGdSHb

This is the Microsoft channel for videos related to the various DevOps tools, practices and services. Subscribe to learn more about the vast Microsoft technologies such as Azure DevOps, GitHub & GitHub Actions, cloud native technologies, all while delivering greater DevOps capabilities in Azure! 

DevOps is not only a product but a practice, a culture and a mindset. Most importantly, it is for everyone! DevOps allows you to plan, execute and iterate your work in a way that delivers a higher quality product to your customer and increases value to your entire organization. DevOps methodologies enable collaboration and transparency. Here we’ll deliver content on the various technologies that enable DevOps on Azure.

videoTitle: What is Containerization in Devops explained
videoDescription:
Defined by the placement of a software component and its environment, dependencies, and configuration into an isolated unit called a container, containerization makes it possible to deploy an application consistently on any computing environment, whether on-premises or cloud-based.

videoTitle: لو لسه متخرج .. إزاي تدخل عالم DevOps خطوة بخطوة؟ مع أحمد فاضل
videoDescription:
في الحلقة دي بنناقش كل التفاصيل المهمة عن عالم DevOps. هنتكلم عن اللي كان بيحصل في تطوير البرمجيات قبل ظهور الـ DevOps وإزاي غير المشهد تمامًا. كمان هنوضح الفرق بين DevOps وSRE، وليه بعض الشركات بتفضل تستخدم واحدة عن التانية. لو بتسأل عن فرص الشغل في المجال ده سواء في مصر أو بره، هتعرف حجم الفرص وأهميتها ومتوسط الرواتب اللي ممكن تحققها. الحلقة كمان بتجاوب على سؤال مهم: إيه اللي محتاج تعمله كخريج جديد عشان تبدأ في المجال؟ وهتديك نصايح عملية لكل اللي محتاج تكون مجهزه قبل ما تروح الإنترفيو. متفوتش الفرصة دي لو عايز تبدأ مشوارك في DevOps بخطوات ثابتة وواضحة.

لينك أكونت ضيفنا أحمد فاضل: 
https://www.linkedin.com/in/ahmed-fadel-30526192?utm_source=share&utm_campaign=share_via&utm_content=profile&utm_medium=android_app

لينك جروب الفيس بوك الحاص بينا:
https://www.facebook.com/groups/1690247508447723/?ref=share&mibextid=lOuIew

videoDescription:
What stuck with me from this chat with Nana Janashia is that curiosity and hunger for learning & sharing will bring you further than anything else.

In her case, learning Kubernetes and AWS and sharing that knowledge eventually lead to becoming Docker Captain, AWS Hero & CNCF Ambassador.

Those were never goals, but always a result of focussing on efforts.
In doing so she managed to grow a YouTube community of 700k subs.

videoTitle: How to Create a CI/CD Pipeline in Azure DevOps
videoDescription:
In this video, you’ll learn how to build a CI/CD pipeline in Azure DevOps from scratch! This tutorial will guide you through the entire process, helping you set up continuous integration and continuous delivery to automate your software development workflow.

This video has everything you need, whether you're new to DevOps or looking to refine your pipeline setup.

Timestamps:

00:00 – Introduction: What is CI/CD and why it's important
03:10 – Setting Up a Project in Azure DevOps: Step-by-step guide to creating a new project
07:40 – Connecting to a Git Repository: Version control setup with Git in Azure Repos
12:25 – Creating the Build Pipeline (CI): Configuring and automating builds
20:50 – Running Tests in the Pipeline: Integrating automated tests in your CI pipeline
27:30 – Creating the Release Pipeline (CD): Automating deployments for continuous delivery
35:15 – Deploying to Azure: Connecting your pipeline to deploy your application to Azure
41:50 – Monitoring and Managing Pipelines: Best practices for monitoring and troubleshooting
48:30 – Best Practices for CI/CD Pipelines: Tips to optimize your DevOps pipeline
54:10 – Conclusion: Final thoughts and next steps
By the end of this video, you'll have a fully functional CI/CD pipeline up and running in Azure DevOps, ready to streamline your development and deployment process!

videoTitle: Understand DevOps in Under 8 Minutes | #devops | Matrix Bricks
videoDescription:
DevOps is one of the most talked-about practices in tech today, but many still aren’t clear on what it actually means. In this video, Mehul Brahmbhatt, Founder and CEO of Matrix Bricks, explains DevOps in simple, practical terms for anyone new to the concept.
Whether you're a developer, product manager, or just curious about how modern software teams work, this video covers the fundamentals in under 8 minutes.

💡 Why This Video Is a Must-Watch:
⦿ Clearly explains what DevOps is and why it exists
⦿ Helps you understand how development and operations work better together
⦿ Breaks down the major challenges DevOps solves in software delivery
⦿ Covers essential principles like automation, CI/CD, and continuous feedback
⦿ Offers insight into how real teams use DevOps to improve speed and quality
 
🛠️ What You’ll Walk Away With:
⦿ A beginner-friendly understanding of DevOps without technical jargon
⦿ A grasp of how CI/CD pipelines help teams deploy faster and more reliably
⦿ Knowledge of the four key pillars that drive successful DevOps practices
⦿ A clearer view of how DevOps improves collaboration across teams
⦿ The confidence to start learning or applying DevOps in your own work

This is a quick overview designed to give you a clear understanding of the topic—no prior experience required.
📌 If you're building digital products or working in tech, understanding DevOps is essential to keeping up with how software is built and delivered today.

videoTitle: ♾️🛠️CI/CD Pipeline Explained in Tamil🔁 | #thillaithetechie
videoDescription:
✅ Continuous Integration (CI):
✅ CI is a development practice where code changes are automatically built, tested, and integrated into a shared repository multiple times a day.
✅ It ensures early detection of integration errors and promotes collaboration among developers by providing rapid feedback on code changes.

✅ Continuous Delivery (CD):
✅ CD extends CI by automating the deployment process, enabling teams to release software changes to production or staging environments quickly and reliably.
✅ It streamlines the deployment pipeline, reduces manual intervention, and enhances the consistency and repeatability of software releases.

✅ Pipeline Automation:
✅ CI/CD pipelines automate various stages of the software delivery process, including code compilation, testing, artifact creation, and deployment.
✅ Automated pipelines ensure consistency, efficiency, and reliability, allowing teams to focus on delivering value-added features and improvements.

✅ Feedback Loop:
✅ CI/CD pipelines incorporate feedback loops at each stage, enabling teams to identify and address issues promptly, iterate on solutions, and continuously improve software quality and performance.

✅ DevOps Culture:
✅ CI/CD practices are integral to the DevOps culture, fostering collaboration between development and operations teams, breaking down silos, and promoting a culture of continuous improvement and innovation.

#devops #automation #tamilshorts

videoTitle: DKT88 - DevOps Mock interview #6 (Senior/Lead DevOps engineer)
videoDescription:
Очередное мок-собеседование на канале! Сегодня в гостях Саша (не СТО) — Senior DevOps Engineer, с которым мы проходим по всему циклу разработки: от проектирования CI/CD до траблшутинга в Kubernetes. 
Много практики, лайв-кодинга и архитектурных вопросов.
Слушаем, запоминаем вопросы, влетаем за разбором на Patreon/Бусти!

🔍 ЧТО СПРАШИВАЛИ

- CI/CD: проектируем идеальный пайплайн для Java-бэкенда и спорим о порядке стейджей
- Git Internals: навигация по дереву коммитов (Detached HEAD) и правка истории
- Kubernetes: архитектура (Cluster API, Control Plane), чем на самом деле занимается Controller Manager и нюансы StatefulSet
- Troubleshooting: чиним права доступа (RBAC) в кластере и разбираем упавший Docker-билд с пропавшими пакетами
- Helm & IaC: зачем нужны Library charts, шаблонизация и структура Terraform-репозитория

⏱️ НАВИГАЦИЯ ПО ВИДЕО

00:00:00 - 🎬 Вступление и приветствие
00:01:15 - 👤 Представление гостя: Саша, Senior DevOps
00:02:37 - 📈 Карьерный путь: от SysAdmin к DevOps и K8s
🚀 ЧАСТЬ 1: CI/CD И Git

00:51:58 - Topology Spread Constraints и Affinity
00:53:49 - Задача: Troubleshooting K8s (CrashLoopBackOff и RBAC)

https://gotopia.tech/articles/agile-is-dead-long-live-devops

Lars Kruse - Technology Counselor & Rainmaker at Inc Inc
Malte Foegen - COO at wibas
Klaus Bucka-Lassen - Free Radical at Netcetera & Agile Coach, Trainer & Keynote Speaker @danish.kauboy 

DESCRIPTION
Agile, Lean, and DevOps are more than buzzwords even though they have taken over the world at different times. The processes and technologies they employed have helped improve the entire world, not just the software world. Klaus Bucka-Lassen, Lars Kruse, and Malte Foegen debate the intersection and cross-pollination between the three worlds with a focus on applying them on all levels in practice in large organizations.

videoTitle: DKT91: Мок-интервью DevOps - Архитектура AWS, Terraform и Live Debug K8s
videoDescription:
Проверяем знания кандидата на позицию Senior DevOps инженера в прямом эфире. В этом выпуске: архитектурные паттерны в AWS, вечный спор Terraform против CloudFormation, глубокое погружение в Kubernetes (Karpenter, скейлинг) и Live-траблшутинг сломанного Helm-чарта.
О ЧЁМ ВЫПУСК:
• Архитектура и облака: Как выбрать между EKS и ECS/Fargate и настроить безопасное хранение бэкапов в S3. 
• IaC войны: Честное сравнение Terraform и CloudFormation — где заканчивается удобство и начинается боль. 
• Kubernetes под капотом: Разбираем Control Plane, работу контроллеров и нюансы обновления on-prem кластеров. 
• Live Debug: Реальная задача по починке упавшего пода (CrashLoopBackOff) — работа с пробами, портами и Helm. 
• CI/CD стратегии: Строим идеальный пайплайн с GitHub Actions и ArgoCD.

ГОСТЬ:
Максим — DevOps-инженер (5 лет опыта DevOps, 10 лет SysAdmin).
Стек: AWS, Terraform, Kubernetes, Ansible, Monitoring.

videoTitle: DKT87 - Разбор Technology Radar #33: AI, MCP, Context Engineering и будущее DevOps
videoDescription:
ThoughtWorks Technology Radar #33 —  обзор трендов IT-ишечке! Разбираем ключевые техники, инструменты и платформы, которые изменят вашу работу в 20265 году. От pre-commit hooks до vLLM — всё, что нужно знать DevOps об AI.

О ЧЁМ ВЫПУСК:
• AI Infrastructure Orchestration — GPU-кластеры, NVIDIA DCGM, RDMA и почему это важно для каждого
• MCP (Model Context Protocol) — эра AI-агентов и интеграции с внешними системами
• Context Engineering — кладезь ресурсов от Anthropic для работы с LLM
• Pre-commit Hooks + Continuous Compliance — защита от утечек секретов и антипаттернов AI
• Sidecar-less Service Mesh (Istio Ambient) — MTLS без оверхеда
• Vibe Coding в Hold — почему Shadow IT ускоряется через AI и чем это грозит
• ARM, Graviton 5, VLLM, Crossplane, N8N и другие инструменты в Adopt/Trial/Assess

Если вы не знаете, что такое Context Engineering, Agent MD или Spec-driven Development — этот выпуск для вас. Прямо сейчас нужно догонять!

videoTitle: DKT92 - MCP - это было ошибкой? Разбираемся и показываем демо
videoDescription:
Наконец-то мы приготовили MCP!

В этом выпуске разбираем Model Context Protocol от Anthropic - протокол, который стал USB-C для AI-систем. Начинаем с базовых building blocks AI (токены, мультимодальность, отсутствие памяти у моделей, RAG, агенты), а потом погружаемся в MCP: архитектура, компоненты (Host, Client, Server), три составляющих MCP-сервера (Tools, Resources, Prompts) и почему это изменило экосистему AI-инструментов.

Демо:
- Obsidian + MCP - получаем to-do лист из ежедневной заметки
- Terraform + Checkov MCP - сканируем AWS Goat на уязвимости
- Kubernetes MCP - troubleshoot и автоматический fix pending/crashloop подов

Обсуждаем главную проблему MCP - context overloading (380K токенов на все AWS MCP серверы!) и вопросы безопасности при выборе MCP-серверов.

НАВИГАЦИЯ:
00:00 - Интро, книжка вышла в печать!
02:13 - Новогодние резолюции и SMART-цели
05:32 - Виктор сдал CKAD - все 3 Kubernetes-сертификата!
07:24 - AI Building Blocks: токены, токенайзер, Hugging Face
10:00 - Мультимодальность моделей
22:22 - У моделей нет памяти! Как работает контекст
27:09 - RAG - обогащение контекста внутренними документами
29:53 - Агенты, мультиагенты, A2A от Google
35:01 - MCP - что это и зачем? USB-C аналогия
42:16 - Архитектура MCP: Host, Client, Server, Tools/Resources/Prompts
46:29 - Безопасность MCP и миграция SSE в HTTP Streaming
46:47 - MCP в CNCF! Новая ветка AI
49:01 - ДЕМО 1: Obsidian + MCP (to-do list)
54:31 - ДЕМО 2: Terraform + Checkov MCP (сканирование AWS Goat)
01:01:02 - ДЕМО 3: Kubernetes MCP (troubleshoot + fix подов)
01:08:26 - Проблема: Context Overloading (10% контекста без единого запроса!)
01:11:30 - Итоги и AI нужно оседлать, а не уклоняться

videoTitle: GitHub vs GitLab: Which DevOps Platform is Best for Your Project?
videoDescription:
The ultimate fight for the modern developer workflow comes down to two giants: GitHub and GitLab. They both start with Git, but their fundamental philosophies on how you build, test, and deploy software couldn't be more different. Are you better off with GitHub's flexible "Best-of-Breed" Marketplace approach, or is GitLab's unified, "All-in-One" DevSecOps platform the right choice for your enterprise?

In this comprehensive comparison, I break down the core features, CI/CD platforms (GitHub Actions vs. GitLab CI/CD), security models, and hosting flexibility to help you make the definitive decision for your team, whether you're working on open-source, a complex enterprise pipeline, or a heavily regulated project.

Watch the full video to find out which platform is the absolute best fit for your specific needs!

What do you think? Which platform are you using for your current project and why? Let me know in the comments below!

If you enjoy the video,  like it, and subscribe to my channel!
See you in the next video!

videoTitle: DevOps Topologies 10 years on: What have we learned about silos, collaboration, and flow?
videoDescription:
Keynote at The DEVOPS Conference Global - London, on March 2024: DevOps Topologies 10 years on: What have we learned about silos, collaboration, and flow?

----
 
In this talk, Matthew Skelton explores the key learnings from 10 years of Team Topologies and State of DevOps Reports and outlines key steps that organizations can take to improve IT delivery performance:

- Organize for flow
- Address team cognitive load
- Estimate the cost of “tangled software”
- Use the Accelerate metrics and community techniques to find good boundaries for flow 
- Develop secure foundations in social and technical practices that support fast flow
- Take a realistic view about the nature and scale of change needed

By combining learnings from State of DevOps Reports and Team Topologies together with principles championed by the DevOps community, organizations can navigate a route to business agility via a fast flow operating model.

----

Speaker: Matthew Skelton, Co-author of Team Topologies, and CEO at Conflux

Matthew Skelton is co-author of the award-winning and ground-breaking book Team Topologies, and Founder & Principal at Conflux. The Team Topologies book was rated one of the ‘Best product management books of all time’ by Book Authority and is widely used by organizations worldwide to transform the way they deliver value.

Matthew is one of the foremost leaders in modern organizational dynamics for fast flow, drawing on Team Topologies and related practices to support organizations with transformation towards a sustainable fast flow of value and true business agility. 

A Chartered Engineer (CEng), Matthew brings together principles and practices from multiple disciplines for a holistic approach to digitally-enriched operating models. He combines his experience as a leader and software architect in multiple contexts (GOV.UK, ecommerce, financial services, telecoms, pharma, retail, robotics, etc.) with a strong interest in the human side of organizations for a compassionate and humanistic approach to organizational effectiveness. #DevOps #thedevopsconference 

----

👇 Learn more:
https://www.thedevopsconference.com/global?utm_campaign=The%20DEVOPS%20Conference%2022&utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&utm_content=speaker-video-tdoc-global-2024

If you'd like to learn more about DevOps transformations, check out our page on the topic here:
https://www.eficode.com/devops-transformation?utm_campaign=DevOps&utm_source=youtube&utm_medium=social&utm_content=speaker-video-tdoc-global-2024-devops-transformation-pillar-page

videoTitle: Create Managed Azure DevOps Pools with Terraform
videoDescription:
Today we're working with Azure Pipelines and exploring the different options you have when working with agent pools. The main focus will be on the new Azure Managed DevOps Agent Pools, what they are, how they work, and why you might want to use them.

We’ll start by deploying some prerequisites using Terraform, including a virtual network to host our agents. You’ll see how to use the AzAPI provider to get the job done and I’ll walk you through it all, step by step, as we build this Terraform project from scratch.

Once everything is up and running, we’ll connect our agent to an Azure Pipeline and run a deployment to test it out.

If you’re looking to automate Azure DevOps agent pool provisioning with Terraform and AzAPI, or just want to learn more about CI/CD in Azure, you’re in the right place. Hope you enjoy the video!

0:00 Intro
1:00 Differences between managed devops pools and other offerings

33:30 Deploying our code
34:10 Create pipelines and test our pool

videoTitle: Understanding the DevOps Threat Matrix | Defender for Cloud in the Field #34
videoDescription:
In this episode of Defender for Cloud in the Field, Ariel Brukman joins Yuri Diogenes to talk about the DevOps Threat Matrix. Ariel talks about the process of creating a new threat matrix for a very complex domain such as DevOps, what it was found during the research process and how the research evolved to create this threat matrix. Ariel also talks about how to use the threat matrix to improve your DevOps defenses, and he gives examples of some common attacks against DevOps environments. 

02:49 - The research leading to DevOps Matrix publication
05:35 - Threats in the execution phase
08:50 - Privilege escalation phase
13:00 - Common patterns of attack
13:42 - Recommendations to build better defenses

Learn more: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/04/06/devops-threat-matrix/

videoTitle: How to architect for continuous delivery? | Romano Roth | The DEVOPS Conference - Global 2023
videoDescription:
Talk at The DEVOPS Conference - Global 2023: HOW TO ARCHITECT FOR CONTINUOUS DELIVERY?

Let's explore the importance of continuous delivery and how you can architect your system for it. 

By taking a step back and looking at the bigger picture, we can design a system that enables us to deliver code changes frequently and reliably. In this talk, Romano Roth covers the key principles of continuous delivery, the benefits it brings, and how to architect your system for it. #DevOps #ContinuousDelivery #CI/CD #TheDEVOPSConference

---

Speaker: In the 20 years where he worked as a software developer, software architect, and consultant for Zühlke, he was able to build up a comprehensive knowledge of software development, architecture, and processes. He has worked with a variety of platforms and technologies, consulting in the sectors of financial, insurance, cyber security, electricity, medical, and aviation. His passion is helping companies bring people, processes, and technology together so that they can deliver continuously value to their customers.

In his free time, Romano is organizing the DevOps Meetups Zürich where the DevOps community of Zürich meets together on a monthly basis. He also organizes the DevOps Days Zürich which is a yearly two-day conference.

videoTitle: SBOM & Security Scanning in CI/CD | Secure Your DevOps Pipeline with GitHub Actions
videoDescription:
🚀 SBOM & Security Scanning in CI/CD | Secure Your DevOps Pipeline with GitHub Actions 🔒

In this video, we take CI/CD security to the next level by integrating SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) generation and advanced security scanning into your GitHub Actions workflow. If you're only scanning container images for vulnerabilities, you're missing hidden risks! 😱

We’ll cover:
✅ What is SBOM & Why You Need It? 📜
✅ How to Generate an SBOM with Syft 🔍
✅ Scanning Dependencies for Vulnerabilities with Grype 🚨
✅ Performing Full Security Scans with Trivy 🛡
✅ Step-by-Step CI/CD Implementation using GitHub Actions

By the end of this video, you'll have an automated security pipeline that ensures complete supply chain security in your CI/CD workflows! 🔥

🔄 Hands-on Demo:
We'll walk through a real-world GitHub Actions pipeline that:
🔹 Generates an SBOM with Syft
🔹 Scans dependencies with Grype
🔹 Conducts a deep security scan on container images with Trivy
🔹 Automates security checks in every code push & pull request

🖥️ Watch as we push a change to GitHub and trigger an automated security scan in real-time!

🔹 What’s Next?
In our next video, we’ll bring everything together into a complete DevSecOps pipeline using GitHub Actions! 🚀

Marcelo Neves é o fundador do CANAL VALOR e trabalha com liderança em desenvolvimento de produtos digitais desde 1993. Nos anos 90 liderou o desenvolvimento de produtos B2C voltados para a saúde. videoDescription:
📢 Connect with me on social media for more career tips and updates:

 - LinkedIn  :  linkedin.com/in/ajay-kumar-5666a6256

Don't forget to like, share, and subscribe! Hit the bell icon to stay updated with our latest videos.

For Interview related training please feel free to reach us over the call 9845421738 to discuss further.

I am gonna tell you what DevOps engineers do in less than a minute with an example.

In a restaurant kitchen, there are chefs and wait staff.

Chefs are responsible for creating and experimenting with new dishes. Their job includes coming up with innovative recipes, testing them, and adding the successful ones to the menu. At the same time, they also prepare food to meet the regular demand of customers who visit the restaurant daily. This dual responsibility of innovation and ongoing service can become overwhelming, especially during busy hours when the kitchen is already at full capacity.

Wait staff, on the other hand, focus on delivering the prepared food to customers. Their primary role is to ensure that the food reaches the diners promptly and in perfect condition, keeping the restaurant’s operations running smoothly.

Experimenting with a new dish during peak hours, however, becomes a significant challenge. The process of testing new recipes while maintaining the flow of regular orders often leads to disruptions in the kitchen, causing delays and dissatisfaction among customers.

To address this, the restaurant hires a kitchen manager. The kitchen manager creates a separate small kitchen dedicated to experimenting with new dishes. This ensures that the chefs can focus on their innovations without interfering with the ongoing operations. Once a dish is perfected, the kitchen manager introduces automation, such as using a conveyor belt, to streamline the cooking process. This allows the new dish to be produced efficiently alongside the existing menu items while ensuring there are no interruptions in serving food to the restaurant's customers.

Yes, DevOps engineers are none other than automation experts.

videoTitle: What's the goal of Platform Engineering? #shorts #devops #platformengineering
videoDescription:
Watch the full video: https://youtu.be/JAblex0uS4k

What’s the goal of Platform Engineering? Platform Engineering focusses on the building the infrastructure and the systems that are needed for running your applications and supporting your application throughout the software life cycle. So,  the goal  is to support the developers in the organisation and treat them as customers. That’s why we have in platform engineering the concept of IDP Internal developer platform.
 
So, this platform is treated as an actual product and the customers of the product are the developers.
What is the role of the IDP?
It is to create a thin layer on top of the toolchain that is used inside the organisation to standardise the processes and help developers to have one way to deploy into production

videoTitle: Day 27 – ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps | 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge
videoDescription:
🚀 Welcome to Day 27 of my 30 Days Azure DevOps Challenge!

🔹 Topic: ARM Templates & Bicep with Azure DevOps

Yesterday, we learned how Terraform brings Infrastructure as Code (IaC) to multiple clouds.
Today, we’re focusing on Azure-native IaC tools:

👉 Key differences:

ARM Templates → JSON files that define Azure resources.

Bicep → A cleaner, simpler language to write the same infrastructure.

✅ Why use them?

Strong native Azure support

Seamless integration with Azure DevOps

Best fit for teams working mainly within Azure

✨ Key takeaway:

Terraform = multi-cloud flexibility 🌎

ARM/Bicep = deep Azure integration & simplicity 💡

📌 Next in Day 28, I’ll cover Monitoring & Logging in Azure DevOps — making sure your apps and pipelines run reliably!

videoTitle: How to Fix High CPU Usage in Production | DevOps Real-Time Scenario (Day 3)
videoDescription:
💥 Day 3 of the DevOps in ARIA Series – Real-Time Scenarios!

🎯 Today’s Interview Question:
“Your production server is showing high CPU usage — what will you do?”

This is more than an interview question — it’s a real-world DevOps challenge you’ll face on the job.

🎥 In this video, I break down:

✅ How to investigate CPU issues using tools like top, htop, Grafana, and Prometheus

✅ How to identify which process or service is consuming resources

✅ When to restart a service — and what to check before and after

✅ How code bugs or deployment issues can spike CPU

✅ When to scale infra or adjust auto-scaling policies

✅ Why documenting and learning from incidents makes you a stronger engineer

💡 Clarity, calmness, and a systematic approach — that’s the DevOps way!

📌 If you're preparing for DevOps interviews or working in production environments, this video will sharpen your troubleshooting skills.

🔗 Watch now, comment your thoughts, and don’t forget to subscribe for Day 4 and beyond!

Lyrics
=====
(Intro)
hello... heLLo... HELLO WORLD?  
Can anybody hear me behind the firewall...?  
404 soul not found...

(Verse 1) 
Push to prod at 3 A.M.,  
Brain’s a loop in mayhem,  
Told myself “just one more line,”  
Now it’s been seven sprints of crime.  

GIT RESET --HARD!!

Don’t forget to update your dependencies...  
You wrote that logic at 2 A.M... it's trash...  
Kevin force-pushed again...

You ever deploy a microservice and pray it doesn’t become a macro-disaster?  
My Docker container’s the only thing holding me together.  
I tried therapy once... then Jira assigned me 14 more tickets.  
My manager said: “Just estimate in story points.”  
I said: haa

(Chorus)
Hello world, hello pain,  
Is this loop recursive or am I insane?  
From Python dreams to Java screams,  
My RAM is low... and so are my hopes.

Agile... agile... agile...  
Deliverable... agile...

(Verse 2) 
Null pointer stabbed me in the back,  
Kubernetes launched a DDoS attack.  
Stack Overflow won’t answer me,  
Even GPT said: “Yo bro, I’d flee.”

BUILD FAILED!!!

(Bridge)
One commit to rule them all...  
One Slack ping begins my fall...  
You're on-call this weekend... forever...

Did you test it?  
It works on my machine!  
Kevin deleted the prod DB!  
Why is prod even accessible from the dashboard?!  
HugOps... HugOps... HugOps...

How did I become this...?  
I just wanted to build an app that makes cat noises...

(Final Chorus)
Ohhhh HELLO WOOORLD, my sanity’s gone,  
These tickets keep respawning at dawn.  
Manager says we’re agile and fast,  
But this sprint’s a dumpster fire blast.

NOT ON PROOOOOOD!!

videoTitle: Stop using Read-Host and do this instead #powershell  #devops  #scripting
videoDescription:
Stop using Read-Host and do this instead:

Read-Host has limitations. 

- You cannot pass information to it 
- You cannot validate what the user provided
- It always stops the script

There is a better way - use parameters instead.

- Control the type of input
- Make it mandatory or optional
- Pass argument from the command line
- or prompt the user to provide an argument, when required and not passed

In this short video, I'll show you how to implement simple parameters and how to use them.

Would you like to learn more about the parameters?

videoTitle: Linux Package Management for DevOps: Stop Breaking Your Deployments
videoDescription:
Linux Package Management for DevOps: Stop Breaking Your Deployments

Package managers are not exciting. But if you’re a DevOps or Kubernetes engineer, they are absolutely foundational.

In this video, you’ll learn Linux package management the way it really matters in production as an ongoing operational responsibility, not a one-time setup task. We’ll connect tools like yum, apt and dnf directly to Ansible, CI/CD pipelines and Kubernetes nodes so you actually understand what you’re automating.

What you’ll learn:
00:00 - Why DevOps engineers must understand package managers
01:01 - Package Management in Real Cloud & Enterprise Environments
01:19 - How DevOps Engineers Actually Use Package Managers (Reality Check)
01:29 - What You’ll Learn in This Video (Scope & Expectations)
01:59 - What Is a Package Manager? (Concept, Not Commands)
02:47 - Where Yum Gets Packages From & What It Really Does
03:15 - RHEL Subscription Manager Explained (Enterprise Reality)
05:33 - When You Don’t Use Subscription Manager (Air-gapped & Internal Repos)
05:52 - Yum Repository Configuration Explained (/etc/yum.repos.d)
07:32 - Core Yum Commands DevOps Engineers Use Daily
11:15 - Production Updates: What NOT to Do & How Updates Really Work
12:14 - Why Blind yum update -y Breaks Production
12:52 - Package Managers Across Distros (Yum vs Apt vs Zypper)
13:37 - Final Takeaway: Why This Layer Matters for Kubernetes & Automation

By the end, you’ll be able to:
- Understand what yum/apt/dnf are doing under your automation tools
- Avoid “works on my machine” package issues in CI/CD
- Debug broken Kubernetes nodes at the Linux layer
- Treat package management as a continuous DevOps responsibility

This video is part of my “Linux for DevOps: From Zero to Hired” series, designed for:
- DevOps engineers
- SREs and Platform engineers
- Developers moving into Kubernetes and cloud-native roles

Next step after package management: learn Linux networking basics, so Kubernetes networking finally makes sense.

If this helped, subscribe for more Linux for DevOps content and Kubernetes-focused breakdowns.

videoTitle: Bash Scripting for DevOps Engineers | Automate Kubernetes, Disk & Log Monitoring
videoDescription:
Bash scripting is one of the most powerful skills every DevOps engineer should know.

In real DevOps environments, engineers repeatedly check things like:
• Is the Kubernetes pod running?
• Is the disk almost full?
• Are there errors in system logs?

Doing this manually once is fine — but doing it every day is not scalable.

This is where bash scripting helps automate repetitive operational tasks.

In this tutorial, you’ll learn the fundamentals of shell scripting and then apply them to real DevOps automation examples used in production environments.

🔧 What You’ll Learn

• How to create and execute a shell script
• Understanding the shebang (#!/bin/bash)
• Setting executable permissions with chmod +x
• Using variables in bash scripting
• Writing if-else conditions for automation decisions
• Using for loops in shell scripts
• Using exit codes for automation workflows

🚀 Real DevOps Automation Scripts

✔ Kubernetes Pod Health Check Script (kubectl + JSONPath)
✔ Disk Usage Monitoring Script with threshold alerts (df, awk, sed)
✔ Real-time Log Monitoring Script using tail -f and grep

These examples demonstrate how small scripts can automate daily DevOps operations like monitoring services, detecting failures, and preventing production issues.

▬▬▬▬▬▬▬ Timestamps ▬▬▬▬▬▬▬
00:00 - Why DevOps Engineers Automate Repetitive Tasks
00:34 - How to Create and Run Your First Shell Script
02:15 - Using Variables in Shell Scripts
03:26 - If Conditions in Bash (Automation Decisions)
06:48 - For Loops in Shell Scripts
08:58 - From Concepts to Real DevOps Automation
09:30 - Script #1: Check Kubernetes Pod Health
12:35 - Script #2: Monitor Disk Usage Automatically
16:09 - Script #3: Detect Errors in Log Files
18:44 - How Small Scripts Power DevOps Automation
19:06 - DevOps Mindset: When to Automate Commands

📌 If you're learning DevOps, Kubernetes, or Linux automation, mastering bash scripting is one of the most valuable skills you can develop.

videoTitle: Bash Scripting for DevOps Engineers | Automate Kubernetes, Disk & Log Monitoring
videoDescription:
Bash scripting is one of the most powerful skills every DevOps engineer should know.

In real DevOps environments, engineers repeatedly check things like:
• Is the Kubernetes pod running?
• Is the disk almost full?
• Are there errors in system logs?

Doing this manually once is fine — but doing it every day is not scalable.

This is where bash scripting helps automate repetitive operational tasks.

In this tutorial, you’ll learn the fundamentals of shell scripting and then apply them to real DevOps automation examples used in production environments.

🔧 What You’ll Learn

• How to create and execute a shell script
• Understanding the shebang (#!/bin/bash)
• Setting executable permissions with chmod +x
• Using variables in bash scripting
• Writing if-else conditions for automation decisions
• Using for loops in shell scripts
• Using exit codes for automation workflows

🚀 Real DevOps Automation Scripts

✔ Kubernetes Pod Health Check Script (kubectl + JSONPath)
✔ Disk Usage Monitoring Script with threshold alerts (df, awk, sed)
✔ Real-time Log Monitoring Script using tail -f and grep

These examples demonstrate how small scripts can automate daily DevOps operations like monitoring services, detecting failures, and preventing production issues.

▬▬▬▬▬▬▬ Timestamps ▬▬▬▬▬▬▬
00:00 - Why DevOps Engineers Automate Repetitive Tasks
00:34 - How to Create and Run Your First Shell Script
02:15 - Using Variables in Shell Scripts
03:26 - If Conditions in Bash (Automation Decisions)
06:48 - For Loops in Shell Scripts
08:58 - From Concepts to Real DevOps Automation
09:30 - Script #1: Check Kubernetes Pod Health
12:35 - Script #2: Monitor Disk Usage Automatically
16:09 - Script #3: Detect Errors in Log Files
18:44 - How Small Scripts Power DevOps Automation
19:06 - DevOps Mindset: When to Automate Commands

📌 If you're learning DevOps, Kubernetes, or Linux automation, mastering bash scripting is one of the most valuable skills you can develop.

Using the Pulumi platform, teams can build, deploy, and manage modern cloud applications faster and with more confidence, using any language, any architecture and any cloud.

videoTitle: I Tried Learning DevOps Only From Documentation — Most People Would Quit
videoDescription:
Everyone tells you to watch tutorials to learn DevOps.

That’s not how I learned it.

In this video, I share my real DevOps learning method — exactly how I used to learn tools when I was starting out:
• Opening official documentation
• Following it step by step
• Breaking things
• Fixing real errors
• Learning how engineers actually think

This is a raw 1-hour session using Jenkins official documentation — no courses, no scripts, no shortcuts, no AI.

You’ll see:
• How beginners actually get stuck
• What documentation explains well (and what it doesn’t)
• Why pipelines fail even when you “follow the steps”
• How real DevOps engineers debug without answers

⚠️ This is NOT a polished tutorial
⚠️ This is NOT a crash course

Today, we dive into AWS CloudFormation, Amazon’s Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service that lets you define and provision AWS resources using templates. If you’re already learning Terraform, CloudFormation will give you another powerful perspective on automating cloud infrastructure.
In this session, you will learn:
• What is AWS CloudFormation & why use it
• CloudFormation vs Terraform (key differences)
• Writing your first YAML CloudFormation template
• Creating AWS resources with CloudFormation (EC2, S3, IAM)
• Deploying a stack in AWS Management Console & CLI
• Parameters, Outputs, and Template structure explained
• Best practices for CloudFormation templates

videoTitle: SRE, DevOps, DevSecOps, Cloud Engineering, and Platform Engineering - What's the difference???
videoDescription:
The key differences between SRE, DevOps, DevSecOps, Cloud Engineering, and Platform Engineering are explained in this comprehensive video! Are you confused about these popular tech roles and methodologies? We break down each concept, explaining its core principles, responsibilities, and how they relate to one another.

In this video, you'll learn:

* **Site Reliability Engineering (SRE):** Focus on reliability, performance, and efficiency of production systems using software engineering principles.
* **DevOps:** A culture and set of practices that aims to break down silos between Development and Operations teams, fostering collaboration and automation.
* **DevSecOps:** Integrating security practices into every stage of the DevOps lifecycle, making security a shared responsibility.
* **Cloud Engineering:** Designing, building, and managing infrastructure and applications on cloud platforms like AWS, Azure, and GCP.
* **Platform Engineering:** Building and maintaining internal developer platforms to streamline software delivery and enhance developer experience.

Whether you're a student, a tech professional looking to specialise, or simply curious about these in-demand fields, this video provides a clear and concise explanation to help you understand the nuances and overlaps between SRE, DevOps, DevSecOps, Cloud Engineering, and Platform Engineering.

Don't forget to like, comment, and subscribe for more tech explainers!

videoTitle: The DevOps Trap Nobody Talks About #shorts
videoDescription:
Most DevOps engineers get noticed only when things break. That work matters — but it rarely gets rewarded.

The real career advantage comes from preventing problems before anyone sees them. Quiet prevention beats loud firefighting.

How to move from Junior to Senior DevOps Thinking:

Master Infrastructure Stability over simple uptime.

Focus on Risk Management and automation.

Build for SRE (Site Reliability Engineering) standards.

#DevOps #CloudEngineering #SRE #EngineeringCareers #TechLeadership #SoftwareEngineering #SystemAxis #SeniorDevOps
channelDescription:
Most infrastructure advice works in controlled demos. It often fails under real constraints.

SystemAxis explores how AI, cloud, and DevOps systems behave in production — under load, under pressure, and under real cost limits.

This channel focuses on practical engineering: stress testing configurations, analyzing bottlenecks, understanding failure modes, and making deliberate architectural decisions. Sometimes that means running controlled experiments. Sometimes it means breaking assumptions. Sometimes it means designing systems properly from the start.

Topics include AI infrastructure, cloud architecture, Linux internals, automation, cost control, platform engineering, and performance tuning.

The goal is simple: understand how systems actually behave - not how they are supposed to behave.

No hype. No demo-only advice. Just production reality.

videoTitle: Linux Firewall Explained for DevOps | firewalld, iptables & Troubleshooting
videoDescription:
Linux Firewall Explained for DevOps | firewalld, iptables & Troubleshooting

Firewall issues are one of the most common causes of production outages in DevOps. Your service is running, DNS works, but traffic still fails. In many cases, the Linux firewall is the root cause.

In this video, you will learn how Linux firewall works using firewalld and iptables, and how packet filtering happens inside the Linux kernel.

This tutorial focuses on real DevOps troubleshooting using practical commands.

Topics covered:

• How Linux firewall packet filtering works
• firewalld explained with real examples
• How to check firewall status and zones
• How to allow ports using firewall-cmd
• iptables explained for DevOps engineers
• INPUT, OUTPUT, FORWARD chains explained
• How Kubernetes uses iptables internally
• DevOps firewall troubleshooting workflow

This video is part of the Linux for DevOps series.

If you want to become strong in Docker, Kubernetes, and DevOps, understanding Linux firewall is essential.

▬▬▬▬▬▬▬ Timestamps  ▬▬▬▬▬▬▬
00:00 - Introduction: Why Firewall Issues Break DevOps Systems
00:33 - How Linux Firewall Packet Filtering Works (Basic Concept)
01:16 - firewalld Tutorial: Check Status, Zones, and Allow Ports
03:00 - iptables Explained: How Linux Actually Filters Packets
03:44 - INPUT, OUTPUT, FORWARD Chains Explained (Visual Guide)
04:18 - DevOps Firewall Troubleshooting Mindset + Outro

  SIEM tools (Splunk, ELK, QRadar)
  Identity & Access Management (IAM, Okta)
  Zero Trust Security Model
  Cloud Security (AWS Security Hub, Azure Defender)
  Ethical Hacking, Penetration Testing

 Roles:
  Security Analyst
  Cybersecurity Engineer
  SOC Analyst
  Cloud Security Architect

3. Cloud Computing & DevOps

 Technologies/Skills to Learn:
  Cloud Platforms: AWS, Azure, GCP
  Docker, Kubernetes, Helm
  Terraform, Pulumi (Infrastructure as Code)
  Jenkins, GitHub Actions, ArgoCD (CI/CD)
  Monitoring: Prometheus, Grafana, ELK
  Linux, Bash, YAML

 Roles:
  Cloud Engineer / Architect
  DevOps Engineer
  Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
  Platform Engineer

4. Data Engineering & nalytics

 Technologies/Skills to Learn:
  SQL, Python (Pandas, NumPy)
  Apache Spark, Apache Kafka
  Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift
  Airflow, dbt (Data Orchestration)
  Power BI, Tableau, Looker
  Data Lake, Delta Lake, Data Warehousing

 Roles:
  Data Engineer
  BI Analyst
  DataOps Engineer
  Analytics Engineer

5. Full Stack Development & App Engineering

 Technologies/Skills to Learn:
  Frontend: React.js, Next.js, Angular
  Backend: Node.js, Spring Boot, Django
  REST & GraphQL APIs
  JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Python
  CI/CD, Docker, Testing frameworks (Jest, JUnit)
  Databases: PostgreSQL, MongoDB, Redis

 Roles:
  Full Stack Developer
  Frontend Developer
  Backend Developer
  App Developer
  Technical Lead

videoTitle: Mobile DevOps Release Management | Permission settings for Admins, Developers and Testers
videoDescription:
Welcome to our Release Management for iOS playlist! Release Management is available to all team members of an app's team, but not everyone can access all functions. Use the Release Manager role to keep your release process secure and reliable.

Here's what we'll cover in this video:
